name: Kit's Chocolate Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the chocolate over hot water, then let it cool until only warm. 2.
While cooling the chocolate, whip the cream until it forms medium-stiff peaks. Do not overbeat or the mousse will lose its smooth, light texture. 3. Separate 4 eggs; set the whites aside. Combine yolks with the remaining whole eggs in an electric mixer. Beat until thick and lemon-colored (about 5 minutes). In a clean copper or stainless steel bowl, whisk egg whites until they begin to stiffen. Gradually add confectioner's sugar and beat until firm peaks form. 4. Quickly combine the cooled melted chocolate with whipped cream and yolks, stirring until smooth. Add remaining cream; stir in liqueur and vanilla. Fold in egg whites until just blended. 5. Pour into serving dishes or one larger dessert dish, then chill at least 4 hours before serving (longer chilling intensifies flavor). The mousse may be frozen up to 2 weeks. Thaw in refrigerator overnight before serving. VARIATION: Use Brownie Cookie Crust from the Brownie Cookies recipe and fill with mousse, chilling at least 4 hours before topping with whipped cream.
